step_description: Secondary redo queue analysis on SQLDR-01
narrative_hint: SalesDB is the critical database. It is generating log at 8,420 KB/s but the DR secondary can only redo at 2,840 KB/s — a deficit of 5,580 KB/s. At this rate the redo queue will NOT catch up; it will continue growing. The secondary disk latency of 42ms is the bottleneck — at that latency the disk I/O throughput caps out at approximately 2,840 KB/s for sequential log writes. The secondary's storage tier is insufficient for the current log generation rate. Estimated catchup time of 84.7 minutes assumes the primary stops generating log — which it won't. Without intervention the redo queue will grow indefinitely. Immediate actions: (1) investigate DR storage I/O, (2) consider throttling bulk operations on primary, (3) evaluate storage upgrade on SQLDR-01.
